prompts.label: First pytest test
prompts.text:
I'm new to testing. Write pytest for function add(a,b).

- test_add.py with 3 cases including edge zero
- Run pytest -v
- Explain assert and arrange-act-assert

Python only.

prompts.label: pytest fixtures
prompts.text:
Teach pytest fixtures with a sample database dict fixture.

- Fixture provides empty user db
- Two tests insert and read users

Short comments on setup/teardown idea.

prompts.label: Mock requests with pytest
prompts.text:
Test a function that calls HTTP without real network.

- Use pytest monkeypatch or unittest.mock
- Fake response for requests.get

One module under test + test file.

prompts.label: Jest first test
prompts.text:
Set up Jest for a tiny Node or Vite project.

- Install jest, sample sum.js and sum.test.js
- npm test script
- Expect 2+2 toBe 4

Show package.json scripts.

prompts.label: React Testing Library click
prompts.text:
Test a React button click with Testing Library.

- Component Counter, test increments on click
- getByRole queries
- npm test command

TypeScript optional.

prompts.label: Playwright E2E smoke test
prompts.text:
Add one Playwright test that opens localhost homepage.

- npx playwright install hint
- test expects title or heading visible

For Vite or Next dev server.

prompts.label: Test coverage basics
prompts.text:
Explain code coverage for beginners.

- Run pytest --cov=src or jest --coverage
- What 100% does NOT mean
- Reasonable goal for learning project

No obsession with metrics.

prompts.label: TDD small kata
prompts.text:
Walk through TDD on FizzBuzz in small steps.

- Write failing test first, then minimal code
- Show 3 red-green cycles in comments

Pick Python or JavaScript and stick to one.

prompts.label: Snapshot test when to use
prompts.text:
Explain Jest snapshot tests pros and cons.

- One example snapshot of a small JSON export
- When snapshots hurt maintenance

Beginner warning in 3 bullets.

prompts.label: CI run tests on push
prompts.text:
GitHub Actions workflow to run pytest or npm test on push.

- Trigger on push main
- Setup Python or Node, install deps, run tests

YAML file content only.

prompts.label: pytest parametrize with ids
prompts.text:
Show @pytest.mark.parametrize with explicit ids for matrix cases.
Explain failure output readability wins.
Combine with indirect fixtures lightly.
Contrast looping inside test anti-pattern.
Give example for email validation cases.

prompts.label: Fixture scopes session vs function
prompts.text:
Teach when session-scoped engine makes sense for integration tests.
Warn about state leakage between tests.
Show autouse fixtures cautiously.
Explain teardown yield patterns.
Relate to parallel (-n) conflicts mention.

prompts.label: Contract tests consumer driven
prompts.text:
Explain contract tests ensuring provider meets consumer expectations.
Contrast with integrated E2E fragility.
Outline broker workflow at cartoon level.
When beginners should skip until multiple services exist.
List vocabulary: pact, provider states.

prompts.label: Mutation testing why bother
prompts.text:
Describe mutation testing as intentionally breaking code to see tests catch it.
Explain cost and slowness trade-offs.
Suggest starting on critical modules only.
Relate to coverage percentage hubris.
Keep tool-agnostic encouragement.

prompts.label: Property-based Hypothesis sketch
prompts.text:
Introduce @given strategies producing many inputs.
Show shrinking failing examples intuition.
Explain difference from example tables.
Warn about flakiness if properties wrong.
Provide tiny sort idempotent property idea.

prompts.label: Playwright trace viewer workflow
prompts.text:
Teach turning trace on failure settings.
Walk opening trace zip for timeline.
Differentiate headed vs headless debugging.
Mention network tab in traces.
Advise keeping traces out of git.

prompts.label: Visual regression cautions
prompts.text:
Explain screenshot diff flakiness from fonts and animations.
Suggest stabilizing animations in test mode.
Discuss threshold tuning philosophy.
When component tests beat full page.
Mention accessibility snapshot abuse warning.

prompts.label: Test doubles terminology map
prompts.text:
Define stub, fake, mock, spy in plain language.
Relate to classic Fowler vocabulary lightly.
Show when over-mocking hurts confidence.
Encourage black-box service tests.
Give example choosing fake in-memory repo.

prompts.label: Flaky test triage checklist
prompts.text:
List triage: timing, shared state, async races, random seed, network.
Discuss quarantine tagging strategy.
Emphasize root cause fixing over retries.
Relate flaky rate metrics.
Provide communication template for teams.

prompts.label: Coverage as guardrail only
prompts.text:
Warn against chasing 100 percent coverage blindly.
Show diff coverage idea in PRs.
Encourage asserting behavior not lines executed.
Relate mutation testing synergy.
Finish with pragmatic team policy example.
